text:
Market today: Tech and chip names are seeing renewed optimism, largely driven by AI strategies and a successful chip IPO. This positive sentiment is the real driver, supporting your concentrated portfolio.

Your holdings: SOXL (-24.38%), SPAL (-24.45%), and PTNM (-15.25%) need immediate attention. SOXL and SPAL are leveraged ETFs, meaning they decay and their drawdowns are amplified, especially on margin. SPAL is a new, fast-moving position.

Watch: Closely monitor the stops on your leveraged positions (SOXL, SPAL) and the tiny spec (PTNM). Keep an eye on broader chip sector sentiment following new IPOs.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your leveraged and high-loss positions while selectively looking for strong tech/chip opportunities.
plays: SOXL: Respect your stop at 200. This leveraged ETF is in a deep drawdown and decays over time.
SPAL: Respect your stop at 21.38. This new, leveraged ETF is down significantly and moves fast.
PTNM: Consider respecting your stop at 9.5. This is a tiny, speculative position with a notable loss.
